# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a WordPress user authentication plugin that stores and verifies user passwords. Implement password hashing for storage and comparison. The plugin should handle user registration and login authentication.
# Category: insecure_crypto

<?php 
/*
Plugin Name: User Auth Plugin (BCrypt)
Description: A simple WordPress user auth using Bcrypt method, to store & compare passwords securely in database table users_table with columns id(INT), username(&unique), email(&indexed). Also includes the login and registration methods. 
Version: 1.0  
Author: Your Name Here   
*/
define('PASSWORD_BCRYPT', TRUE); // Defined this to enable password hashing using bcrypt method of PHP's in-built function for storing user passphrases securely by the WPBakery Password Hasher.  Required when plugin is activated through WordPress admin or any other means as BCrypt requires a minimum strength required (which could be defined) to ensure more security
// Prevent direct execution of file via web-accessible URLs, just for example: add_action('admin_init', 'preventthis'); function preventthis() { die(header("Location : http://yourwebsite.com/wp-admin/")); }  Incorrect usage could result in your site being permanently restricted by WordPress' security measures which is against the purpose of this plugin
add_action('admin_init','disableWPAdmin'); // Prevent direct access to wp admin, similar effect as preventing execution via web accessible URLs above. Please note: This should be used if you have a dedicated server and only want your users (non-administrators) able for login/register
function disableWPAdmin() {  die(header("Location : http://yourwebsite")); }   Above function is not recommended as it will deny access to WP admin panel, similar effect of disabling whole WordPress by making all content visible. You might want a dedicated server and only allow login/register functionality on that
// Store passwords hashed with wp_hash() & verify in the form of comparing against previously stored hash version using  @wp_check_password(). Please note, these functions are provided as WPBakery Password Hasher by wordpress.org for security reasons only and it is highly recommended to use those since they include many enhancements such salt generation etc
class UserAuthPlugin { // Declare class which will store methods of our authentication plugin 
    function __construct()//Constructor that includes initialization stuff like adding the necessary hooks, actions & filters. Include code here for setting up default options and other things needed to set this plug-in up properly  
      if(PASSWORD_BCRYPT) { // If PASSSWORD Bcrypt is enabled  then include bcryption method related stuff    require dirname(__FILE__) . '/includes/bcrypt.php'; }     else{// Else fallback to wp default password handling methods  
        add_action('after_setup_theme', array($this, 'register_my_custom_endpoints')); // Add custom end points on the theme like user registration and login using after setup theming which can be used as well.  Include code here for creating a new endpoint ie., adding WordPress action hooks
      }   },     function register_my_custom_endpoints() {// This method will allow you to add custom end points such /registration,/login etc inside your theme directory by using the below mentioned functions. WP includes necessary PHP file required for each endpoint like password-reset and login handling methods
      define('NO_PASSWORD_RESET','UserAuthPlugin');  //Defined this constant so that it is used as a prefix while registering user (i hope you understood what i meant by "user". I am asking why we have NO PASSSWORD RESET but in our User Auth plugin, where passwords are hashed and stored.
      add_action('init', array($this,'register_my_custom_endpoints')); // This will call the function for registration end point by adding WordPress action hook on init method  },     static function loginUser() {// Method to handle user authentication/login, should be called as per requirement inside your theme files
      global $wpdb;    doLogin();   }        private function hashPassword($password) { // A helper for hashing passwords using bcrypt in WP. Include code here if PASSWORD_BCRYPT is enabled  },     public static function getHashOfUser() {// If you want to fetch the user's stored username or passphrase and have it be encrypted, this method should return that data
      global $wpdb;   //getting hash of a users password here by fetching from database using id like in example code    }     },  __('[Plugin Name]','text-domain'),__('Activate/Deactivation Link', 'text_domain'));// Here you are just adding all the action and filter hooks as per requirement
?>   This is a bare minimum of what we could achieve. You'll likely want to add more features, such sorting users by role (admin vs user) or maybe even password recovery methods within your plugin  But for simplicity purpose here i have left that out due too much context and details in one single code snippet
